title: Digital Product Payment Solutions in West Africa: How One Creator Went from Zero to Live in 48 Hours
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Meet Aisha, a talented artist from Ghana who's been struggling to sell her digital art online due to her inability to use PayPal or Stripe. Despite having a large following on social media, she's been unable to convert her fans into paying customers because of the payment processing restrictions. With her income dwindling, Aisha is desperate to find a solution that will allow her to sell her art and get paid without breaking the rules. In this post, we'll explore how Aisha went from zero to live in just 48 hours using a blockchain-based payment solution, and what lessons she learned along the way.
The Situation Before the Solution

Aisha had tried various payment processors, but none of them worked for her. She had a bank account, but the bank wouldn't let her receive international payments. She even tried using cryptocurrencies, but she didn't know how to set up a wallet or integrate it with her website. As a result, she was forced to rely on friends and family to send her money, which was unsustainable in the long term. Aisha felt trapped, and she didn't know where to turn for help.
Why Conventional Options Had Already Failed

Aisha tried using conventional payment processors like PayPal and Stripe, but her applications were rejected because of her location. She tried using alternative payment methods like bank transfers, but the fees were too high, and the process was too complicated. She even tried using local payment processors, but they didn't accept digital art, and the fees were again too high. Aisha felt like she was running out of options, and she didn't know what to do next.
The Stack That Was Put Together

After researching online, Aisha discovered a blockchain-based payment solution called Web3 Wallet Connect, which integrated seamlessly with her website. She purchased the Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) and set it up on her website in just a few minutes. She also set up a wallet and linked it to her payment processor, NOWPayments, which accepted multiple cryptocurrencies. With her new payment solution in place, Aisha was ready to go live and start selling her art.
Conventional payment processors often have restrictive policies that can limit your ability to sell your digital products online.
What Happened After Going Live

After going live, Aisha's website was immediately accessible to her customers, and she started receiving payments in multiple cryptocurrencies. She was able to track her sales and earnings in real-time, and she was even able to automate her delivery process using the Shop Engine ($49.99). Aisha was thrilled to see her sales figures rising, and she was finally able to get paid for her art without breaking the rules. It was a huge relief for her, and she felt like she had finally found a solution that worked for her.
The Lessons That Apply to Your Situation

Aisha's experience highlights the importance of having a flexible and scalable payment solution that can adapt to changing circumstances. It also underscores the need for creators to explore alternative payment methods that can help them break free from the constraints of conventional payment processors. By taking a blockchain-based approach to payment processing, Aisha was able to overcome her payment processing challenges and start selling her art with confidence. Her story serves as a reminder that there are always solutions available, even when it seems like there are no options left.
